title: Snake & Ladders — Original
subtitle: the original Indian Moksha Patam
goalMeaning: Liberation. Free at last from the wheel of desire and rebirth.

ladders.name: Shraddha
ladders.meaning: Faith steadies the seeker and lifts the heart toward the light.

ladders.name: Daana
ladders.meaning: What you give away freely returns to you as ascent.

ladders.name: Vinaya
ladders.meaning: Bow low, and you are raised high.

ladders.name: Satya
ladders.meaning: Truth is a straight ladder; it never lets you slip.

ladders.name: Kshama
ladders.meaning: To forgive is to set down a great weight and rise.

ladders.name: Tapas
ladders.meaning: Steady effort burns away the dross and carries you upward.

ladders.name: Jnana
ladders.meaning: Right knowledge clears the last mists on the path to liberation.

snakes.name: Moha
snakes.meaning: Attachment clouds the mind and drags you back to where you began.

snakes.name: Mada
snakes.meaning: Drunk on the senses, you stumble and lose your footing.

snakes.name: Matsara
snakes.meaning: Envy of another's climb only lowers your own.

snakes.name: Steya
snakes.meaning: What is taken unjustly must be repaid with a fall.

snakes.name: Asatya
snakes.meaning: A single lie is a slippery slope; down you slide.

snakes.name: Krodha
snakes.meaning: Rage burns the one who holds it, and casts you far down.

snakes.name: Lobha
snakes.meaning: Greed swallows the greedy; the higher you were, the harder you fall.

snakes.name: Ahankara
snakes.meaning: Pride goes before the fall, even near the very summit.

snakes.name: Kama
snakes.meaning: Desire seizes you at the threshold of liberation and casts you to the depths.
